issue_title: Clicking email confirmation links in Outlook results in a "stale link" error
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug When using the UPDATE_EMAIL feature, updating your email address in the account console and then clicking on the verification link in some email clients (at least Outlook) results in an error: The email address is still successfully updated, but the error message might be confusing for the user. --- The issue is caused by Outlook "Safe Links" [sending a HEAD request](https://community.wappler.io/t/dealing-with-head-requests/33802), which already verifies the email address. The GET request from the browser then results in a EXECUTE_ACTIONS_ERROR with reason "staleEmailVerificationLink". The verification link upon initial registration also has this issue, but in that case doesn't result in a user-visible error, because the VERIFY_EMAIL_ERROR with error "email_already_verified" still results in an automatic redirect back to the application, just as a successful VERIFY_EMAIL would. ### Version 26.2.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior HEAD requests to the `/realms//login-actions/action-token` endpoint should not cause any state change on the server. ### Actual behavior HEAD requests to the `/realms//login-actions/action-token` endpoint complete the action and invalidate the token. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Enable the UPDATE_EMAIL feature and the corresponding "Update Email" required action. 2. Open the account console and log in to an existing account. Click the "Update email address" button 3. Enter a valid email address and click "Submit" 4. Open the email in Microsoft Outlook and click the link. (This only works with publicly accessible Keycloak instances on the internet, as the HEAD request originates from Microsoft's servers). If you don't have Outlook or your Keycloak instance isn't public, you can instead copy the verification link and manually send a HEAD request using a tool of your choice, e.g. `curl -I `, and then open the link in your browser. 5. You will see "stale link" error message. ### Anything else? Keycloak event log: nginx log: You can see the HEAD request from a Microsoft IP arriving first and getting a 200 response, then the GET request from my browser arriving second and getting a 400 response. --- The responsible code should be this: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ef312b570c2ed0243242b93688016d7aaf6d91fd/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/LoginActionsService.java#L532-L549 It seems that if no dedicated `@HEAD` handler is defined, the `@GET` handler is called instead (which in this case has side effects). There is an argument to be made that neither HEAD nor GET requests should actually cause an action to complete, because that violates the assumption of them being [safe](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Safe/HTTP) and [idempotent](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Idempotent). But to solve that, the verification link would have to always show a form with a button that sends a POST request. To solve this issue, it would be enough for HEAD requests to not execute the action token handler.
commit_message:
Skip processing HEAD requests for action tokens

Closes #41834

issue_title: memberOf attribute empty or values with a DN that does not match the role base DN fetches all roles
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

We have encountered a security issue with syncing LDAP groupOfNames entities to Keycloak roles with role-ldap-mapper. We have created simplest scenario that you could use to reproduce.

Issue occurs when we try to sync users that has memberOf attribute with some value outside the role DN. Using simply cn=test would lead to assigning all roles that are synced from LDAP.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

We have encountered a security issue with syncing LDAP groupOfNames entities to Keycloak roles with role-ldap-mapper. We have created simplest scenario that you could use to reproduce.

Issue occurs when we try to sync users that has memberOf attribute with some value outside the role DN. Using simply cn=test would lead to assigning all roles that are synced from LDAP.

### Actual behavior

Do not fetch roles (and groups) if memberOf value is empty of values are using a baseDN other than the base DN configured in the role/group mapper.

### How to Reproduce?

Issue occurs when we try to sync users that has memberOf attribute with some value outside the role DN. Using simply cn=test would lead to assigning all roles that are synced from LDAP.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Unbalanced HTML in login form templates
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

Some login form templates contain unbalanced HTML tags.

E.g.: `update-email.ftl`

Invalid:
```
<button class="${properties.kcButtonClass!} ${properties.kcButtonDefaultClass!} ${properties.kcButtonLargeClass!}" type="submit" name="cancel-aia" value="true" />${msg("doCancel")}</button>
```

Valid:
```
<button class="${properties.kcButtonClass!} ${properties.kcButtonDefaultClass!} ${properties.kcButtonLargeClass!}" type="submit" name="cancel-aia" value="true">${msg("doCancel")}</button>
```

This error can is also present in the following base/login template files:
- base/login/update-email.ftl
- base/login/login-verify-email.ftl
- base/login/login-update-profile.ftl
- base/login/login-update-password.ftl
- base/login/delete-account-confirm.ftl
- keycloak.v2/login/login-config-totp.ftl

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The login form templates should not contain unbalanced HTML tags.

### Actual behavior

Some login form templates contain unbalanced HTML tags.

### How to Reproduce?

Look at the listed template files with a HTML validating editor.

### Anything else?

issue_title: Hibernate 7.1 breaks TiDB support
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

ci

### Describe the bug

The test showed to be unstable in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/16966315188/job/48115619560?pr=41882

```
2025-08-14T18:21:00.1129051Z 2025-08-14 18:21:00,047 WARN  [managed.keycloak] (pool-2-thread-1) [liquibase.changelog.DatabaseChangeLog] (main) Due to mysql SQL limitations, setNullable will lose primary key/autoincrement/not null/comment settings explicitly redefined in the change. Use <sql> or <modifySql> to re-specify all configuration if this is the case
2025-08-14T18:21:37.2154982Z 2025-08-14 18:21:37,209 WARN  [managed.keycloak] (main) [org.hibernate.orm.jdbc.error] (executor-thread-1) HHH000247: ErrorCode: 1146, SQLState: 42S02
2025-08-14T18:21:37.2175552Z 2025-08-14 18:21:37,209 WARN  [managed.keycloak] (main) [org.hibernate.orm.jdbc.error] (executor-thread-1) Table 'test.ue1_0' doesn't exist
2025-08-14T18:21:37.2177621Z 2025-08-14 18:21:37,209 ERROR [managed.keycloak] (main) [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1) Uncaught server error: org.keycloak.models.ModelException: Database operation failed
```

This is caused by the Hibernate 7.1 upgrade, which creates different SQL statements which are not understood by TiDB: 

```
-- Hibernate 7.1.0.Final - fails on TiDB, succeeds on MySQL
select ue1_0.ID from USER_ENTITY ue1_0 where ue1_0.ID=1 for update of ue1_0;

-- Hibernate 7.0.8.Final
select ue1_0.ID from USER_ENTITY ue1_0 where ue1_0.ID=1 for update of USER_ENTITY;
```


### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A deletion of a user should succeed

### Actual behavior

A deletion of a user doesn't work

### How to Reproduce?

Run any test locally, for example UserResourceTypePermissionTest

### Anything else?

issue_title: Backwards incompatible changes to 26.3.0 cause NullPointerException when requesting /certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

**Overview**
After upgrade of Keycloak server from 26.1.2 to 26.3.2, our services started receiving 500 when requesting keypair and certificate generation (POST `/auth/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client}/certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download`).
Our server stack trace indicates a NullPointerException in [ClientAttributeCertificateResource](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/42520d8409847d456870df75cede4993a03a6a61/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/ClientAttributeCertificateResource.java#L346):
```
"exception": {
  "refId":1,
  "exceptionType":"java.lang.NullPointerException",
  "message":"Cannot invoke \"java.lang.Integer.intValue()\" because the return value of \"org.keycloak.representations.KeyStoreConfig.getKeySize()\" is null",
  "frames":[{"class":"org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.ClientAttributeCertificateResource","method":"generateAndGetKeystore","line":346}...
```
This issue happens because of backwards incompatible changes that were introduced in scope of [this PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/40428/files) to solve https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/38620. The problem is that new fields `keySize` and `validity` are not nullable, but they do not have fallback values.
This change was reflected in `keycloak-admin-client` / `keycloak-client-common-synced` since 26.0.6, but previous versions do not support these fields.
So, our services that use `keycloak-admin-client` / `keycloak-client-common-synced` of older versions (26.0.4 / 26.0.5) now receive 500s.

### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Contract of `/auth/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client}/certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download` endpoint is updated in a backwards compatible way to allow users to update server first and then client libraries with no downtime.

### Actual behavior

Contract of `/auth/admin/realms/{realm}/clients/{client}/certificates/jwt.credential/generate-and-download` fail with 500 when new fields `keySize` and `validity` are not populated. This does not allow us to update server first and then client libraries with no downtime

### How to Reproduce?

Request keypair and certificate generation using `keycloak-admin-client` / `keycloak-client-common-synced` on the version prior to 26.0.6 against Keycloak 26.3.2.

```
var config = new KeyStoreConfig();
config.setFormat("PKCS12");
config.setKeyAlias(clientId);
config.setKeyPassword(keyPassword);
config.setStorePassword(keyPassword);

var keystore = clientResource.getCertficateResource("jwt.credential").generateAndGetKeystore(config);
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Check null for new keySize and validity parameters when generating certificates
Closes #41906

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Role mapping `account.manage-account-links` not sufficient for Client initiated account linking
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Role mapping `account.manage-account-links` is not sufficient to perform Client initiated account linking. Trying to link accounts will only result in an `Insufficient permissions to link identities.` error. 

The [dokumentation](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_development/index.html#client-initiated-account-linking) states:

> The user must have an account.manage-account **or** account.manage-account-links role mapping.



### Version

26.3.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Client initiated account linking should be possible with just the `account.manage-account-links` role mapping. 

### Actual behavior

Client initiated account linking is only possible with the `account.manage-account` role mapping.

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the procedure outlined in [Client initiated account linking](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_development/index.html#client-initiated-account-linking`) and only assign the `account.manage-account-links` role mapping.

### Anything else?

The check in [IdentityBrokerService.java](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/IdentityBrokerService.java#L1004) only checks for `AccountRoles.MANAGE_ACCOUNT` and will return an INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSION error if not present.
commit_message:
Check manage-account-links role for client initiated account linking

Closes #41914

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: Check for non-ascii local part on emails depending on SMTP configuration
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

This is a follow-up for #41023. With the changes done for that issue now the SMTP configuration rejects sending emails to addresses that have non-ascii in the local part if the SMTP configuration does not allow UTF-8. This is OK because we are preventing sending messages to incorrect addresses but we have decided to add this validation to the built-in UP email validator. This way the server will also reject the registration of emails that cannot be used by the SMTP server.

We have managed two ideas:

1. Just use the SMTP configuration to control the email and allow non-ascii if SMTP is not configured or allows UTF-8.
2. Add an option in the validator. This option is independent to the SMTP configuration in the realm.

For the moment I will use the option 1 for the PR but this can change after reviews.

### Version

999

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The email with non-ascii chars in the local part should be rejected if the SMTP server configuration does not allow sending them.

### Actual behavior

The email is accepted.

### How to Reproduce?

See description.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Check for non-ascii local part on emails depending on SMTP configuration

Closes #41994

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

On Keycloak 26.2.5 or 26.3.1, every time we deploy a new version of our Keycloak with custom providers, some user sessions are losing their client sessions, even if they are not expired yet. See the following screenshot before a deployment: Then, after the Keycloak pods were restarted, one session lost its client: This is in one of our lower environments, the problem is a lot worse in production, where there are many sessions active. Then, we start to see a lot of `REFRESH_TOKEN_ERROR` in our logs: ``` type="REFRESH_TOKEN_ERROR", realmId="1234", realmName="redacted", clientId="redacted", userId="null", sessionId="4321", ipAddress="redacted", error="invalid_token", reason="Session doesn't have required client", grant_type="refresh_token", refresh_token_type="Refresh", ``` This is our session lifetime configs: We currently have two Keycloak pods running on Kubernetes ### Version 26.3.1 ### Regression - [x] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior The user sessions should not lose their clients after each Keycloak restart, or if this is expected, a clear documentation on why it's needed and how to tweak it. ### Actual behavior Refresh of access tokens fails because user sessions are losing their client sessions during Keycloak restart. This started to happen after we upgraded from Keycloak 26.1.4 to 26.2.5. ### How to Reproduce? Unfortunately, I couldn't find a reliable way to reproduce this other than creating the OIDC sessions and restarting the application manually. ### Anything else? We also observe some logs from `ClientSessionPersistentChangelogBasedTransaction`, like: ``` client-session not imported from persister for sessionId=null, offline=false, removing from persister. ``` **Enabled features:**

issue_title: Dynamic client authentication configuration uses wrong config
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When creating a custom client authenticator to make it configurable on a per-client basis in the admin console it uses configuration from `getConfigProperties` instead of `getConfigPropertiesPerClient` which it should use.

This means that it's not possible to create a custom client authenticator where the authenticator in the authentication flow is configured with some properties, while configured with different properties on a per-client basis.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Config properties defined in getConfigPropertiesPerClient should be available when configuring authenticator for a client

### Actual behavior

Config properties defined in getConfigProperties is available when configuring authenticator for a client

### How to Reproduce?

This can now be reproduced with using the experiemental Federated JWT client authenticator.

Start Keycloak with `kc.sh start-dev --features=client-auth-federated`. Create a new client with authentication and select `Signed JWT - Federated` as the authenticator type. 

According to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/fb35439479e5f6f4fb3788f6f5036592121dceda/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/client/FederatedJWTClientAuthenticator.java#L137-L139 it should be then be possible to set the Identity provider that is should use, but this config option is not visible since the Admin UI is looking at `getConfigProperties` (this is for configuring the authenticator in the flows, not when configuring it for a client) instead of `getConfigPropertiesPerClient`.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Recovery Codes are shown as "another way" even if not configured
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

When using 2FA with default browser flow, the option for "Recovery Codes" will be displayed as an alternative, even if user doesn't have Recovery Codes configured. If the user selects the Recovery Codes to be used, the authentication yields in an error.

### Version

26.3 / nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Recovery Codes will only be offered as an alternative way, if user has configured them. Like the other 2FA options.

### Actual behavior

As soon as a user has configured at least one 2FA option, e.g. OTP and the auth flow has enabled OTP and Recovery Codes as Alternatives for 2FA, the "try another way" link will be shown and the credentials selection screen will offer Recovery Codes, although the user hasn't configured them as credential.

### How to Reproduce?

- enable in default browser auth flow the `Recovery Codes` as an _Alternative_ to OTP
- create a user with password credential
- log in with the user to account console, add the OTP as 2FA
- log out
- log in again with username/password, then, as expected, the OTP screen is shown
- also the link "try another way" will be shown, but shouldn't
- click on _"try another way"_, the options `OTP` and `Recovery Codes` are displayed (must not be, as there is no alternative configured)
- click on `Recovery Codes` -> the error screen occurs

### Anything else?

issue_title: [Operator] Update job incorrectly inherits podTemplate configuration from unsupported.podTemplate
issue_body:
### Description

## Issue Summary

When using the `unsupported.podTemplate` configuration for the main Keycloak pods, the update job incorrectly inherits the same `nodeSelector` and `tolerations` configuration, preventing separate control over update job scheduling.

## Current Behavior
1. Main Keycloak pods use `unsupported.podTemplate` configuration ✅
2. Update job automatically inherits the same `nodeSelector` and `tolerations` ❌
3. No way to configure update job scheduling separately ❌

## Expected Behavior
The Keycloak operator appears to be applying the `unsupported.podTemplate` configuration to both the main StatefulSet and the update job, instead of allowing separate configuration for each.

## Impact
- Update jobs cannot be scheduled on **different nodes** than the main Keycloak pods
- Cannot use different tolerations for update jobs vs. main pods
- Limits flexibility in cluster resource management
- May cause update job failures if the inherited configuration is inappropriate

## Environment
- Keycloak Operator Version: 26.3.3
- Kubernetes Version: 1.33.0
- Use Case: Multi-node cluster with dedicated nodes for different workloads

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Deploy Keycloak with unsupported.podTemplate configuration:
```
apiVersion: k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1
kind: Keycloak
metadata:
  name: keycloak
spec:
  # ... other configuration ...
  unsupported:
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        nodeSelector:
          node-role: "keycloak-dedicated"
        tolerations:
          - key: "dedicated"
            operator: "Equal"
            value: "keycloak"
            effect: "NoSchedule"
```
2. Trigger an update (e.g., change image tag, configuration)
3. Observe that the update job uses the same nodeSelector and tolerations
4. The update job cannot be scheduled because this node group have more than 3 nodes (keycloak used 3 nodes)





### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

_No response_

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: "Session/EntityManager is closed" during  application startup "singleFile" users import
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area testsuite ### Describe the bug > [!IMPORTANT] > This issue does not impact any of the current "testsuite" use cases. But there is a bug in the `DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory`&`EntityManagerProxy` classes. I am working on a continuation of the https://github.com/thomasdarimont/embedded-spring-boot-keycloak-server private fork, which enables an Spring Boot embedded Keycloak instance using the same approach as the "testsuite". While I'm performing realm migration during the server start-up I get `Session/EntityManager is closed` #### Since? The issue got introduced with commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/24910d9e1c76a1e953936c558ab02a27eb91820e which was introduced to Keycloak `v26.3.0`. #### When? The issue occurs if Infinispan XML configuration has properties defined for `infinispan/jgroups/stack-file` and related `infinispan/cache-container/transport`. #### Cause? The introduced feature `nested entity managers` caches a closed entity manager into KeycloakSession's `ENTITY_MANAGER_PROXIES` attribute's collection. During 1. the lazy initialization of [`org.keycloak.connections.jpa.DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory.java#L161) 2. [`DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory::addSpecificNamedQueries`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory.java#L247) gets called which 1. creates the entity manager https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory.java#L97-L103 2. passes it to be cache proxied with [`EntityManagerProxy.create`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory.java#L104) * that persists the created entity manager into [KeycloakSession's attribute `ENTITY_MANAGER_PROXIES` entity managers collection](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/support/EntityManagerProxy.java#L58-L64). 3. and [closes the exact same entity manager](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/DefaultJpaConnectionProviderFactory.java#L124) that was previously attached to KeycloakSession. Later 1. the `DefaultExportImportManager` performs the users import [with nested entity managers feature enabled ny running `EntityManagers.runInBatch`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/model/storage-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/storage/datastore/DefaultExportImportManager.java#L479) 2. that fetches the cached entity managers form KeycloakSession's `ENTITY_MANAGER_PROXIES` attribute. 3. [and tries to unwrap the `org.hibernate.Session`](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/26.3.0/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/support/EntityManagers.java#L105) 4. but the wrapped hibernate session is already closed and it throws `Session/EntityManager is closed` ### Version \>=26.3.0 <=26.3.3 & latest (edbe281) ### Regression - [x] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Realm import migration is successfully performed during Keycloak application bootstrapping. ### Actual behavior ``` java.lang.RuntimeException: RESTEASY003325: Failed to construct issue_title: Dedicated client scope mappers missing
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When using a custom client scope that contains colon (":") in the name, the dedicated client scope mappers are not included in the token response.


### Version

26.3.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Request: 
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--data-urlencode 'client_id=test' \
--data-urlencode 'username=test' \
--data-urlencode 'password=test' \
--data-urlencode 'grant_type=password' \
--data-urlencode 'scope=openid test:create'
```
Token:  
```
{
  "exp": 1756218351,
  "iat": 1756218291,
  ...
  "scope": "openid profile test:create email",
  "dedicated-groups-mapper": [
    "test"
  ],
  ...
}
```

### Actual behavior

Request: 
```
curl --location 'http://localhost:8080/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--data-urlencode 'client_id=test' \
--data-urlencode 'username=test' \
--data-urlencode 'password=test' \
--data-urlencode 'grant_type=password' \
--data-urlencode 'scope=openid test:create'
```
Token:  
```
{
  "exp": 1756218351,
  "iat": 1756218291,
  ...
  "scope": "openid profile test:create email",
  ...
}
```

*NOTE missing dedicated-groups-mapper

### How to Reproduce?

Steps to reproduce

- Create client scope with name **test:create** (notice the ":" in the name)
- Create client with name for example **test**
- Add **test:create** scope to test client
- Add dedicated client scope mapper to test client (any mapper)

When requesting a token with scope "test:create" the dedicated client scope mappers are not included.

This behavior changed in version 26.3.0. Version 26.2.0 works as expected.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Bug in configuration keycoak via keycloak.conf
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Starting from 26.3.2 Keycloak fails during the build stage if the referenced environment variable in the config isn't provided. However, the config property that references env var isn't build-time property.

### Detailed description
By combining two approaches [Referencing env vars](https://www.keycloak.org/server/configuration#_format_for_referencing_environment_variables) and [Category specific log levels](https://www.keycloak.org/server/logging#_configuring_category_specific_log_levels), I have the following config.

**keycloak.conf**
```
log-level=${KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL:INFO},org.keycloak.events:DEBUG
```

`KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL` isn't provided during build time, though present (sometimes not) on runtime.

On version 26.3.1, it's building fine and works as expected - root log level is taken from `KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL` env at runtime, or `INFO` is used as a fallback. After upgrading to 26.3.2 , the build started failing with the message: `ERROR: logging category '${KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL' is not valid`



### Version

26.3.2, 26.3.3, nightly

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Root log level is taken from `KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL` env at runtime, or `INFO` is used as a fallback. No errors appear during build time, even if `KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL` is not preset at build stage.

### Actual behavior

During image build fails with the error:
```
ERROR: Failed to run 'build' command.
ERROR: logging category '${KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL' is not valid
```

### How to Reproduce?

- Create the following config and Dockerfile to build image

**keycloak.conf**
```
db=postgres
log-level=${KEYCLOAK_LOG_LEVEL:INFO},org.keycloak.events:DEBUG
```
**Dockerfile**
```
ARG VERSION
FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:${VERSION} AS builder

COPY keycloak.conf /opt/keycloak/conf/keycloak.conf

RUN /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build

FROM quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:${VERSION}
COPY --from=builder /opt/keycloak/ /opt/keycloak/

ENTRYPOINT ["/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh"]
```
- Build (change `VERSION` to see difference in behavior on different keycloak's versions):  `docker build --build-arg VERSION=26.3.2 -t kc .`
- Change build arg `VERSION` to `26.3.1` - the build will succeed

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Importing SAML IdP metadata sets Validate Signatures to false even if signing certificate is provided
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

When adding a new SAML Identity Provider to a Keycloak realm and importing the IdP's metadata, either using the metadata URL or via the XML import, Validate Signatures is not always set to true even if a signing certificate is provided in the IdP metadata.

In fact, Validate Signatures is only set to true when WantAuthnRequestsSigned is true in the IdP metadata. This doesn't seem correct as that flag relates to SAML requests being signed rather than SAML assertions and responses.

### Version

26.3.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When importing an IdP's metadata, Validate Signatures is set to true if the IdP metadata contains a signing certificate.

### Actual behavior

When importing an IdP's metadata, Validate Signatures is set to true only when the IdP metadata has WantAuthnRequestsSigned set to true.


### How to Reproduce?

1. Set up a SAML identity provider that doesn't require authentication requests to be signed, but does sign assertions or responses.
2. Create a new SAML 2.0 Identity Provider in Keycloak
3. Import the IdP metadata

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Federation Cache Policy details not shown when editing provider in Keycloak 26.3+
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug When editing a custom user federation provider in the Keycloak Admin Console, the Cache Policy field is visible, but the detailed cache policy settings (e.g., interval frequency, eviction options, lifespan) are not displayed in Keycloak 26.3+. This was working fine in previous versions (e.g., 26.2.x and earlier), but since upgrading to 26.3, the cache policy configuration does not take effect after saving. ### Version 26.3.3 ### Regression - [x] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior When I change the Cach policy, the details settings for that policy should be visible. ### Actual behavior The detaiul configuration of cache policy are not showing. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Install Keycloak 26.3+. 2. Deploy a custom User Federation SPI implementation. 3. In the Admin Console → User Federation → select your custom provider. 4. Configure Cache Policy (e.g., EVICT_DAILY,EVICT_WEEKLY etc.) and save. 5. Go to the edit page of this config 6. Observe that the cache policy details config not showing. ### Anything else? - The issue appears to be limited to the Cache Policy UI. - Built-in Federation Settings are working fine.
commit_message:
Fix Federation Cache Policy details not shown when editing provider (#42222) (#45375)

When the SelectControl component was refactored to use ui-shared controls,
the comparison logic for cache policy values was not updated correctly.

The refactored SelectControl returns string values ('EVICT_WEEKLY', 'EVICT_DAILY', etc.)
when using string options, but the code was still comparing against arrays (['EVICT_WEEKLY']).

This caused the conditional rendering of cache policy detail fields (evictionDay,
evictionHour, evictionMinute, maxLifespan) to always fail when editing an existing
federation provider.

Changes:
- Changed direct string comparisons instead of array comparisons
- Updated defaultValue from array to string format
- Removed array indexing from field names (e.g., 'config.evictionDay[0]' to 'config.evictionDay')
- Removed unused isEqual import from lodash-es

Signed-off-by: Roni Saha <roni.cse@gmail.com>

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Response should be much faster. With cache disabled, it consistently takes 4s to get 8000 users.

### Actual behavior

LDAP Federation with Cache Settings = DEFAULT, and all users already synced,
GET /admin/realms/master/users?max=8000 takes more than 3 minutes.
Subsequent responses from cache are fast and take ~1s.

### How to Reproduce?

LDAP instance with large number of users is required. It needs to be added in User Federation. All settings can be left default, except for connection settings in sections `Connection and authentication` and `LDAP searching and updating`.

Cache setting is crucial. Cache needs to be active to reproduce the problem.

It's best to sync users from LDAP into keycloak DB before trying to get users
to avoid LDAP importing noise.

### Anything else?

I generated FlameGraph with VisualVM, but were unable to understand how to fix this cache issue.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cd579e2f-c590-4f52-9afb-3e7ca0eca548)

issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area dist/quarkus ### Describe the bug I have a quarkus.properties file that is not being picked up by Keycloak since 26.3.0. It seems like the an element in the InterceptorChain returns a fallback, even when the next element can resolve an actual value: At this point in the chain it resolves to the fallback value: But if I manually resolve the value on the next entry, it correctly resolves the value from the quarkus.properties file: For context, I observed this issue in the `SmallRyeConfig#getConfigValue` method. ### Version 26.3.3 ### Regression - [x] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Keycloak uses the values configured in quarkus.properties ### Actual behavior Keycloak configures the datasource as a H2 database, because that is the fallback value, even though the ### How to Reproduce? Configure a quarkus named datasource using quarkus.properties file, create an entitymanager for that datasource, and you'll see that the configuration from the properties file is ignored. ### Anything else? _No response_
